commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=85e99023cf0a9a0f326cb30ace6d8263e9d9708d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Linux system-scope systemd unit detection for the gateway service, routes status/restart/stop through the system manager when detected, blocks unmanaged fallback signaling, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +179. Total +269 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the core failure: current main only checks the user unit path and calls `systemctl --user`. The PR body also supplies a live Linux baseline, but I did not independently run the systemd scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A maintainer or contributor should decide whether custom system unit names are supported here, then revise the PR or linked issue scope accordingly.

target.title: fix(daemon): detect system-scope systemd gateway units on Linux (#87577)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #87577 by teaching the Linux gateway service code to recognize **system-scope** systemd units (`/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`, `/usr/lib/systemd/system/...`, `/lib/systemd/system/...`) in addition to the existing user-scope unit. `openclaw status` now reports system-managed gateways correctly, and `openclaw gateway restart` no longer drops to an unmanaged SIGUSR1 against a process the system supervisor owns.

Reuses the system-scope detection patterns already shipping in `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.ts:106` and `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:98` per the clawsweeper review note.

## Behavior change

`src/daemon/systemd.ts` — added `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope(env)` returning `{ scope: "user" | "system", unitPath }`. Three call sites scope their systemctl invocations off of it:

- **`isSystemdServiceEnabled`** — falls through to system-scope `systemctl is-enabled` (no `--user`) when only `/etc/systemd/system/<unit>.service` (or sibling paths) exists. Before: returned `false` whenever the user unit file was absent, regardless of system units.
- **`readSystemdServiceRuntime`** — queries the system manager (no `--user`) when the unit is system-scope. Before: always went through the user manager and surfaced `Failed to connect to bus` on headless servers without a user session.
- **`runSystemdServiceAction` (stop/restart)** — when the unit is system-scope and the caller is not root, throws `\`sudo systemctl <action> openclaw-gateway.service\`` guidance instead of falling through to user-bus systemctl. When the caller *is* root, runs `systemctl restart`/`stop` directly. No silent privilege escalation (per clawsweeper open-question on auto-sudo).

`src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` — `stopGatewayWithoutServiceManager` and `restartGatewayWithoutServiceManager` short-circuit with the same sudo guidance when a system-scope unit is installed, so the unmanaged SIGUSR1/SIGTERM fallback can never reach a systemd-managed gateway process.

User-scope behavior is unchanged.

## Diff size

```
 src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts |  44 +++++++++++
 src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts      |  16 +++++
 src/daemon/systemd.test.ts           | 135 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 src/daemon/systemd.ts                | 114 +++++++++++++++++++++++-----
 4 files changed, 289 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)
```

## Test plan

- [x] New unit tests covering `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` (user-preferred, system fallback in `/etc/systemd/system` and `/usr/lib/systemd/system`, null when missing), `isSystemdServiceEnabled` against an enabled/disabled system unit, `readSystemdServiceRuntime` going through the system manager, and `restartSystemdService` / `stopSystemdService` throwing sudo guidance vs. restarting under root.
- [x] New lifecycle tests covering the unmanaged restart/stop short-circuit when a system-scope unit is installed.
- [x] Focused acceptance suite from the clawsweeper review note (7 files / 211 tests / 206 pass / 5 skipped):
  - `src/daemon/systemd.test.ts`
  - `src/daemon/inspect.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle-core.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`
  - `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.test.ts`
  - `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.test.ts`
- [ ] CI checks + clawsweeper re-review.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: openclaw#87577. After the patch the CLI detects system-scope OpenClaw gateway units, reports them as enabled/running through the system manager, and refuses to SIGUSR1 the systemd-managed process on restart.

Real environment tested: DigitalOcean droplet `ubuntu-s-1vcpu-512mb-10gb-blr1`, Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS, Linux 6.8.0-71-generic, Node v24.15.0 (matches the reporter's Environment block). A fake gateway process listens on port 18789 under `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` running as user `admin` (uid 1000) — same deployment shape as the issue reproducer. Live `systemctl status` confirms `Active: active (running)` with `MainPID 80266 (python3)` and `CGroup: /system.slice/openclaw-gateway.service`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: dropped the system unit at `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service`, ran `systemctl daemon-reload && systemctl enable --now openclaw-gateway`, then exercised the patched helpers (lifted verbatim from `src/daemon/systemd.ts`) via `sudo -u admin node /opt/openclaw-baseline.mjs` (pre-fix logic for comparison), `sudo -u admin node /opt/openclaw-proof.mjs` (post-fix, non-root operator), and `node /opt/openclaw-proof.mjs` (post-fix, root). Each invocation calls `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope`, `isSystemdServiceEnabled`, `readSystemdServiceRuntime`, and `restartSystemdService` and prints the live results.

Evidence after fix:

Live `systemctl status` output captured before exercising the helpers:

```
openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (system-scope test for openclaw#87577)
     Loaded: loaded (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-05-28 11:48:24 UTC; 57s ago
   Main PID: 80266 (python3)
     CGroup: /system.slice/openclaw-gateway.service
LISTEN 0  128  127.0.0.1:18789  0.0.0.0:*  users:(("python3",pid=80266,fd=3))
```

Baseline (current main, run as non-root `admin` — reproduces the issue):

```
=== BASELINE isSystemdServiceEnabled (user-scope only) ===
false
=== BASELINE readSystemdServiceRuntime (user-scope only) ===
{ status: 'unknown', detail: 'Failed to connect to bus: No medium found' }
=== BASELINE restart fallback (would SIGUSR1 unmanaged process) ===
WOULD SIGUSR1 pid(s): 80266 (gateway treated as unmanaged process)
{ result: 'restarted-unmanaged', pids: [ 80266 ] }
```

Post-fix (non-root `admin`):

```
=== findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope ===
{ scope: 'system', unitPath: '/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service' }
=== isSystemdServiceEnabled ===
true
=== readSystemdServiceRuntime ===
{ scope: 'system', unit: 'openclaw-gateway.service', activeState: 'active', subState: 'running', mainPid: '80266' }
=== restartSystemdService (system-scope, non-root expected) ===
threw: openclaw-gateway.service is a system-scope unit (/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service); run `sudo systemctl restart openclaw-gateway.service` to restart it
```

Post-fix (root, the only path that should actually mutate the unit):

```
=== restartSystemdService (system-scope, non-root expected) ===
returned: { outcome: 'completed' }
```

`systemctl show` between the two restart attempts confirms `MainPID` only changed after the root run, never after the non-root run — i.e. no SIGUSR1 leaked through to the systemd-managed gateway process.

Observed result after fix: system-scope OpenClaw gateway units are detected, status reports the real `Active: active (running)` state via the system manager, restart/stop refuse the unmanaged signal path for system-scope units, non-root callers get an actionable `sudo systemctl restart openclaw-gateway.service` hint, and root callers get an actual `systemctl restart` invocation. User-scope deployments are untouched (existing user-scope tests stay green).

What was not tested: a real `openclaw` binary running directly under the system unit. The published 2026.5.26 tarball on npm is missing several runtime deps (`json5`, `tslog`, ...) on a fresh `npm install -g openclaw`, so I exercised the production helpers via a tiny driver that mirrors the new `findInstalledSystemdGatewayScope` / `isSystemdServiceEnabled` / `readSystemdServiceRuntime` / `restartSystemdService` logic verbatim from `src/daemon/systemd.ts`. The driver, the pre-fix baseline driver, and the full artifact set (`00-setup.txt`, `01-baseline-prefix.txt`, `02-postfix-admin.txt`, `03-postfix-root.txt`) live at `/opt/proof-artifacts/` on the droplet and locally at `/tmp/openclaw-87577-proof/`. Happy to re-run against a maintainer-built tarball or to attach the raw artifact files to the PR if preferred.

## Refs

- Fixes #87577
- Reuses existing system-scope patterns from `src/commands/doctor-gateway-daemon-flow.ts:106` and `src/cli/update-cli/restart-helper.ts:98`.
target.author: yetval
